segments.text: Behold now, the place where we dwell with thee is too strait for us.
segments.speaker: Sons of the Prophets

segments.text: Let us go, we pray thee, unto Jordan, and take thence every man a beam, and let us make us a place there, where we may dwell.
segments.speaker: Sons of the Prophets

segments.text: Go ye.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Be content, I pray thee, and go with thy servants.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I will go.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: So he went with them. And when they came to Jordan, they cut down wood.
But as one was felling a beam, the axe head fell into the water:
Alas, master! for it was borrowed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Where fell it?
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And he shewed him the place. And he cut down a stick, and cast it in thither; and the iron did swim.
Take it up to thee.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And he put out his hand, and took it.
Then the king of Syria warred against Israel,
In such and such a place shall be my camp.
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: Beware that thou pass not such a place; for thither the Syrians are come down.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And the king of Israel sent to the place which the man of God told him and warned him of, and saved himself there, not once nor twice.
Therefore the heart of the king of Syria was sore troubled for this thing;
Will ye not shew me which of us is for the king of Israel?
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: None, my lord, O king: but Elisha, the prophet that is in Israel, telleth the king of Israel the words that thou speakest in thy bedchamber.
segments.speaker: Syrian Servant

segments.text: Go and spy where he is, that I may send and fetch him.
segments.speaker: King of Syria

segments.text: And it was told him, saying, Behold, he is in Dothan.
Therefore sent he thither horses, and chariots, and a great host: and they came by night, and compassed the city about.
And when the servant of the man of God was risen early, and gone forth, behold, an host compassed the city both with horses and chariots.
Alas, my master! how shall we do?
segments.speaker: Elisha's Servant

segments.text: Fear not: for they that be with us are more than they that be with them.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: LORD, I pray thee, open his eyes, that he may see.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: The LORD opened the eyes of the young man; and he saw: and, behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire round about Elisha.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Smite this people, I pray thee, with blindness.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: He smote them with blindness according to the word of Elisha.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: This is not the way, neither is this the city: follow me, and I will bring you to the man whom ye seek. But he led them to Samaria.
segments.speaker: Elisha

footnotes: follow…: Heb. come ye after me

segments.text: LORD, open the eyes of these men, that they may see.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: The LORD opened their eyes, and they saw; and, behold, they were in the midst of Samaria.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: My father, shall I smite them? shall I smite them?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: Thou shalt not smite them: wouldest thou smite those whom thou hast taken captive with thy sword and with thy bow? set bread and water before them, that they may eat and drink, and go to their master.
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: And he prepared great provision for them: and when they had eaten and drunk, he sent them away, and they went to their master. So the bands of Syria came no more into the land of Israel.
And it came to pass after this, that Ben-hadad king of Syria gathered all his host, and went up, and besieged Samaria.

And there was a great famine in Samaria: and, behold, they besieged it, until an ass's head was sold for fourscore pieces of silver, and the fourth part of a cab of dove's dung for five pieces of silver.
And as the king of Israel was passing by upon the wall, there cried a woman unto him,
Help, my lord, O king.
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: If the LORD do not help thee, whence shall I help thee? out of the barnfloor, or out of the winepress?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

footnotes: If…: or, Let not the LORD save thee

segments.text: What aileth thee?
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: This woman said unto me, Give thy son, that we may eat him to day, and we will eat my son to morrow.
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: So we boiled my son, and did eat him: and I said unto her on the next day, Give thy son, that we may eat him: and she hath hid her son.
segments.speaker: Woman

segments.text: And it came to pass, when the king heard the words of the woman, that he rent his clothes; and he passed by upon the wall, and the people looked, and, behold, he had sackcloth within upon his flesh.
God do so and more also to me, if the head of Elisha the son of Shaphat shall stand on him this day.
segments.speaker: King of Israel

segments.text: See ye how this son of a murderer hath sent to take away mine head? look, when the messenger cometh, shut the door, and hold him fast at the door: is not the sound of his master's feet behind him?
segments.speaker: Elisha

segments.text: Behold, this evil is of the LORD; what should I wait for the LORD any longer?
segments.speaker: Messenger
